title: Removing and installing the steering rack end

blocks.text: Removing and installing the steering rack end

items: When the gearbox is secured with a clamp, it shall be held in place by a piece of wood or similar material at the flange.
Keep removed parts free from dust and dirt.
Refer to the steering gearbox development diagram for the work to be performed.

blocks.text: 1. Steering gear box removal
2. Tie rod end removal
Remove the tie rod ends (A) and lock nuts (B).
3. Rack end boot removal
Remove the boot band (A). Remove the tie rod clips (B) and remove the boots (C).

items: After removing the boot, make sure that there is no muddy water or debris in the housing of the rack and steering gear box and inside the boot.

blocks.text: 4. Steering rack end removal
Fix the gearbox mount (A) to the workbench with a clamp (or a commercial tool such as a Shako vise) (B) and a piece of wood (C).

items: Do not clamp the cylinder cylindrical part of the gearbox. It may cause deformation or damage.

blocks.text: Causes the lock washer (A) to bend.
Loosen rack end (A). Remove rack end, lock washer (B) and spacer (C).

items: When securing the gearbox to the clamp, it shall be held in place by a piece of wood or similar material at the flange.
Refer to the steering gearbox development diagram for the work to be performed.

blocks.text: 1. Steering rack end mounting
Fix the gearbox mount (A) to the workbench with a clamp (or a commercial tool such as a Shako vise) (B) and a piece of wood (C).

items: Do not clamp the cylinder cylindrical part of the gearbox. It may cause deformation or damage.

blocks.text: Attach spacers (A) to the rack ends. Install a new lock washer (B) on the rack end (C) and screw the rack end onto the rack end. Tighten the rack end to the specified torque.
Caulk the lock in the groove (A) at the end of the rack to prevent it from turning.
Apply grease (Maltemp ACJ) to the boot attachment area (A) of the rack end.
2. Rack End Boot Mounting
Clean the boot mounting surface (A) on the housing side and apply grease (Molywhite LSG). Install the boots (B) and secure with the tie rod clips (C).

items: Boot mounting groove must be free of grease, moisture, dust, etc.

items.text: Boots are securely installed in the mounting groove.
Boots are securely installed in the mounting groove.
Do not allow grease to adhere to the rack end threads (D). If grease does adhere, be sure to wipe it off.
Do not allow grease to adhere to the rack end threads (D). If grease does adhere, be sure to wipe it off.
No kinks or dents in the boots.
No kinks or dents in the boots.

blocks.text: Wrap the new boot band (A) around the boot mounting groove. Hook the pawl (C) of one band end into the hole (B) of the band end.

Tighten the protrusion (A) on the boot band with a band pincher (commercially available tool). Move the rack from side to side and inspect the boots for kinks, crushing, or loose bands. If the boot mount rotates, retighten the boot band caulk.
3. Tie rod end mounting
Install the tie rod ends (A) and tighten the lock nuts (B).
4. Steering gearbox installation
